description: Build the Account Intelligence Notes UI: account-level and prospect-level note-taking with intelligence signals, timeline, and framework dimensions. Schema already exists (20260315000000_create_accounts_intelligence.sql).

userStories.title: Add Accounts route, page, and nav item
userStories.description: As a user, I need an Accounts page in the main navigation so I can view and manage company accounts that group my contacts.
acceptanceCriteria: ROUTES.ACCOUNTS = '/accounts' added to routes.ts
ROUTES.ACCOUNT_DETAIL = (accountId) => `/accounts/${accountId}` added
AccountsPage.tsx created with list of accounts fetched from Supabase accounts table
Each row shows: name, domain, contact count, last signal date
Nav item 'Accounts' added to AppLayout.tsx after 'Lists'
Route registered in App.tsx wrapped in ProtectedRoute
Empty state shows 'No accounts yet — create your first account' with CTA
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow existing page patterns (Lists.tsx, Contacts). Use org_id pattern from NEXT_SESSION.md. Accounts table already has RLS.

userStories.title: Create Account modal with name, domain, industry
userStories.description: As a user, I need to create new accounts so I can group contacts under a company and start tracking intelligence.
acceptanceCriteria: CreateAccountModal.tsx with fields: name (required), domain, industry, employee_count_range
Modal opens from AccountsPage 'Create Account' button
On submit, inserts into accounts table with org_id and created_by from auth context
Duplicate name within org shows validation error (unique constraint)
After creation, account appears in list without page reload (invalidate query)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow CreateSalesBlockModal.tsx pattern. Use existing Modal.tsx and shadcn/ui form components.

userStories.title: Build AccountDetailPage with tabs layout
userStories.description: As a user, I need an account detail page with tabs for Intelligence, Contacts, and Timeline so I can manage all account information in one place.
acceptanceCriteria: AccountDetailPage.tsx renders at /accounts/:accountId
Page header shows account name, domain, industry, employee range, edit button
Three tabs: Intelligence (default), Contacts, Timeline
Intelligence tab is initially empty with placeholder
Contacts tab shows contacts where account_id matches, with count badge
Timeline tab shows timeline_events for this account, sorted by created_at DESC
Back navigation to /accounts
404 state if account not found or wrong org
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow ContactDetailPage.tsx pattern for tabs and layout. Route already defined in ACCT-001.

userStories.title: Build account-level intelligence signals panel
userStories.description: As a user, I need to add and view account-level intelligence signals (business context, problem fit, pain evidence, buying readiness, etc.) so I can build a strategic picture of the account.
acceptanceCriteria: IntelligenceSignalsPanel.tsx component used in AccountDetailPage Intelligence tab
Shows existing signals grouped by signal_type, filtered by level='account'
Each signal card shows: signal_type label, content, confidence (1-5 stars), source badge, created_at
Add Signal button opens inline form with: signal_type dropdown (account-level types only), content textarea, confidence slider (1-5), evidence_reference optional text
Signal is saved to intelligence_signals table with account_id, org_id, level='account', created_by
Edit and delete actions on own signals
Timeline event created on signal_created
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Signal types for account level: business_context, problem_fit, pain_evidence, economic_impact, buying_readiness, stakeholder_coverage. Create timeline_event on insert.

userStories.title: Link contacts to accounts with purpose classification
userStories.description: As a user, I need to link existing contacts to an account and classify their purpose (prospect, intel source, internal champion) so I can map the stakeholder landscape.
acceptanceCriteria: Contacts tab on AccountDetailPage shows linked contacts with purpose badges
Link Contact button opens a searchable contact picker (contacts in same org without an account_id)
When linking, user selects contact_purpose from dropdown: prospect, intel_source, internal_champion
Contact's account_id and contact_purpose are updated in contacts table
Unlink action sets account_id to null and contact_purpose to 'unknown'
Contact count on AccountsPage list updates after linking
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: contact_purpose enum: prospect, intel_source, internal_champion, unknown. Show classification_changed timeline event when purpose changes.

userStories.title: Build prospect-level intelligence signals on ContactDetailPage
userStories.description: As a user viewing a contact's detail page, I need to add prospect-level intelligence signals (role relevance, influence level, engagement, etc.) so I can track what I know about each individual.
acceptanceCriteria: ProspectSignalsPanel.tsx component added to ContactDetailPage below existing Contact Notes section
Shows signals where contact_id matches AND level='prospect'
Signal types: role_relevance, influence_level, problem_awareness, relationship_strength, engagement
Each signal shows: type label, content, confidence, source badge, date
Add Signal inline form same pattern as account-level panel
Prospect classification dropdown: champion, influencer, connector, blocker, low_relevance
Classification stored on the signal's classification column
Only shows if contact has an account_id (otherwise shows 'Link to an account to track intelligence')
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Prospect signals require account_id on the contact. Show a CTA to link contact to account if not linked.

userStories.title: Build framework dimension notes (SPIN/BANT/MEDDIC)
userStories.description: As a user, I need to capture framework-specific notes (e.g. BANT: budget, authority, need, timeline) against an account so I can systematically qualify opportunities.
acceptanceCriteria: FrameworkNotesPanel.tsx component added to AccountDetailPage Intelligence tab (below signals)
Organization's default_framework determines which framework dimensions to show
BANT shows: Budget, Authority, Need, Timeline sections
SPIN shows: Situation, Problem, Implication, Need-Payoff sections
MEDDIC shows: Metrics, Economic Buyer, Decision Criteria, Decision Process, Identify Pain, Champion sections
Each dimension is an editable text area that saves as an intelligence_signal with signal_type='fw_{framework}_{dimension}'
Framework selector in account header allows switching (updates org default if changed)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Framework types stored as fw_bant_budget, fw_spin_situation etc. in intelligence_signals. If no default_framework on org, prompt user to choose one.

userStories.title: Build account timeline feed
userStories.description: As a user, I need to see a chronological timeline of all intelligence activity on an account (signals created, classifications changed, framework reviews) so I can track the account's evolution.
acceptanceCriteria: AccountTimelinePanel.tsx component used in Timeline tab of AccountDetailPage
Fetches timeline_events for the account, ordered by created_at DESC
Each event shows: icon by event_type, title, body preview, actor name, timestamp
Events grouped by date (Today, Yesterday, March 15, etc.)
Clicking a signal_created event navigates to that signal in the Intelligence tab
Pagination with 'Load more' after 20 events
Empty state: 'No activity yet — add your first intelligence signal'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow ContactActivityTimeline.tsx pattern. Event types: signal_created, signal_updated, signal_deleted, classification_changed, engagement_event, research_note, ai_suggestion, framework_review.
